title: YouTube script
description: Machine-read source of truth for a YouTube video script, at projects/{slug}/video/{lang}/{slug}.script.json. The narration is segmented into roles (hook/intro/segment/cta/outro); the human-facing teleprompter doc is rendered from this into a sibling .md. The deterministic script gate (scripts/check-script.mjs) enforces structure (hook first, a CTA) and pacing (estimated runtime from narration word count / wpm within tolerance of target_seconds; hook short enough). Topic selection should reuse the SEO keyword-research entry point — never invent search demand.

keyword.description: The target keyword/topic (ideally backed by a *.research.json with decision: pursue).

target_seconds.description: Intended video length in seconds. The gate checks estimated runtime is within tolerance of this.

words_per_minute.description: Speaking rate for the runtime estimate. Default 150 (gate). Use ~125 for slow/educational delivery.

max_hook_seconds.description: Cap on the hook's estimated length. Default 15 (gate).

sections.description: Ordered script beats. The FIRST section must be role 'hook'; at least one section must be role 'cta'.

role.description: hook = the first ~10s grab; intro = context/value promise; segment = a body beat; cta = call to action; outro = sign-off.

title.description: Short label for this beat (shown as a heading in the rendered doc).

narration.description: The spoken words. Word count drives the runtime estimate.

visual.description: Optional b-roll / on-screen note (NOT counted toward runtime).
